behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is rule logic + config lookup inside the scoring model; no behavior text requires cross-module handoff, real OS/network/subprocess, or AI output. Higher levels were rejected because the behavior is explicitly about a single threshold computation in the scoring model.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring function with an in-memory config containing merchant-category thresholds and a transaction just above threshold; assert the exact numeric score contribution and final score.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model’s fraud judgment over context/patterns, not deterministic orchestration. A stubbed model would always pass and hide real misclassification failure. Workflow was rejected because this behavior describes one model decision, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on representative transaction-context fixtures (including rapid small multi-geo purchases with sub-threshold rule values) and assert fraud probability/label crosses the fraud decision threshold at acceptable quality.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Likely failure is at module handoff between scoring logic and transaction-history access/window filtering (time boundaries, per-user scoping). No concrete requirement for real OS/network/subprocess, so not System. No AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scoring service to a local in-process repository (or test DB) with seeded timestamps around window edges; assert per-user counts and resulting velocity contribution are correct for inclusive/exclusive boundaries.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: This is an explicit cross-module invariant: combining outputs from two models into one hold decision. Unit could miss wiring/mapping errors between model outputs and decision layer. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no real infra dependency and no need to validate model output quality, only OR orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the decision/orchestration path with scoring result=not flagged and risk result=flagged (stubbed model outputs), then assert transaction state becomes 'held_for_review'. Add converse cases to prevent accidental AND logic.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is response composition/contract across modules (scoring output + risk output + reviewer payload). Unit tests on a mapper alone may miss dropped/renamed fields in actual wiring. Not System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior checks presence and propagation of fields, not real infra or model-quality decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the aggregation endpoint/service with deterministic model outputs and assert combined payload schema includes `risk_score`, `fraud_probability`, and per-model flag reasons mapped correctly for reviewer consumption.
